caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: OEWS role mix is 4-digit 5418 and includes advertising/media-rep contamination rather than a pure 541820 workforce.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Residual roles are not observed and drive much of the estimate.
inputs.pi_dist: No reopened source provides a NAICS-specific measurement of AI savings pass-through.
inputs.pi_moat: Protection varies sharply by crisis, regulated and generalist subsegment.
inputs.t50_years: Professional use is a proxy for effective adoption at NAICS 541820 establishments.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Survey respondents are PR professionals, not a random US NAICS-541820 establishment sample.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is an explanatory analogy, not an observed financial series.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No NAICS-specific owner-age study was found in reopened sources.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of a reopened source is not evidence that no shortage exists.
inputs.active_consolidators: The source covers sector transactions, including larger agencies and non-US activity.
inputs.buy_mult: S6 is a commercial broad marketing-agency table, not disclosed NAICS-541820 private transactions; earnouts can make headline multiple non-cash-at-close.
inputs.exit_mult: Multiple support is broad-sector and must be replaced with process-specific indications in diligence.
inputs.pricing_structure: No NAICS-wide pricing survey was reopened.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $271,021 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541820); class <5: 6,209 firms, avg revenue/firm $468,889 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $73,381 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 927 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,829,861 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $286,373 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 551 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,315,470 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $518,871 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 348 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,294,121 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,611,030 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 73 firms, avg revenue/firm $38,745,397 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,063,655 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 49 firms, avg revenue/firm $82,824,510 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,962,036 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 421

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI use is already widespread in PR, but role fractions retain senior counsel, judgment and relationship work. The 4-digit occupational proxy requires an explicit residual estimate.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541820 ($9,020,215,177) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541820 ($16,903,608,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics pass and the investment thesis is directionally supported. The record is publishable with its authored population, proxy, valuation, ownership and uncertainty caveats preserved.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS United States Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Official 541820 scope: establishments designing and implementing PR campaigns, including lobbying, political consulting and PR consulting.

sources.title: Communicators embrace tools like AI and LinkedIn at rapid pace

sources.title: Study reveals rising application of AI across communications by the public relations industry
sources.what_it_supports: USC Annenberg description of agency and corporate AI application in communications work.

sources.what_it_supports: Commercial transaction guide describing PR-agency client-relationship transfer risk and typical earnout structure.

sources.title: 2025 M&A Review: Fewer Deals But Bigger Deals
sources.what_it_supports: PR-sector 2025 deal activity, larger-seller concentration, PE activity and buyer demand for technology/data capability.

sources.title: Marketing Agency EBITDA Multiples & Valuations – 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Commercial broad marketing-agency EBITDA multiple table, segmented by EBITDA size, used only as a disclosed valuation proxy.

title: Public Relations Agencies
